item.title: China blocks Meta’s $2bn purchase of AI group Manus
item.description: Regulators had reviewed whether deal violated Beijing’s investment rules

item.title: China warns EU over proposed ‘Made in Europe’ law
item.description: Beijing threatens unspecified retaliation over fears that bill to strengthen bloc’s industry will hurt its companies

item.title: Italy approves extradition of alleged Chinese hacker to US
item.description: Xu Zewei accused of industrial espionage over suspected effort to steal Covid-19 vaccine secrets

item.title: Goldman Sachs raises oil price forecast as war disruption drags on
item.description: Analysts expect Brent crude to trade at about $90 in fourth quarter, up from earlier $80 prediction

item.title: The tensions behind Palantir’s efforts to transform English healthcare
item.description: Senior NHS staff divided on practical benefits of US company’s ‘not terrible, not revolutionary’ software

item.title: The Future of Marketing
item.description: Time runs out for ‘Mad Men’ as AI moves in; live sporting events offer marketers an expanding and valuable audience; online personalities offer access to a new generation of customers; marketers face risks and rewards on social media

item.title: Britain’s stealth fighter project faces 10-week funding deadline
item.description: BAE Systems, one of the defence groups involved, says it will ‘redeploy’ staff if longer-term funds not secured

item.title: Prosecute Russian-sponsored saboteurs, Estonia tells Europe
item.description: Countries must do more to deter Moscow’s shadowy campaign to recruit marginalised people to carry out attacks, says Estonian spy chief

item.title: UK ministers resist alignment with EU’s AI rules
item.description: Officials are concerned about the damage to the country’s technology sector as well as its alliance with the US

item.title: Anthropic investors pay sharply different fees for access
item.description: Plus, Vanguard warns on prediction markets, private credit’s borrowing strain and Henry Moore at Kew

item.title: Large UK companies in the dark about how their data is used overseas by AI
item.description: Survey of senior technology and data executives finds lack of understanding about how information is handled abroad

item.title: Can London win back home buyers from the UAE?
item.description: The effect of the Middle East war on Dubai’s property market has been instant. But better financial incentives are needed for wealthy expats to choose the capital over Monaco or Geneva

item.title: Healthcare is driving America’s economy
item.description: This is a huge industry — and that’s not necessarily a good thing

item.title: Inside China’s plans to fight in space
item.description: From seizing satellites to striking Earth from orbit — Beijing is developing dual-use capabilities in an intensifying arms race with the US

item.title: Historic climate shipping deal faces ‘real fight’ as talks restart
item.description: Negotiators accused US last year of using ‘bully-boy tactics’ to sink a UN-backed net zero framework for the industry

item.title: Private equity backers raise new conflict concerns over sweetheart deals
item.description: Investors question if some institutions rubber-stamp deals that could benefit other businesses

item.title: Consumer brands take the leap into influencer marketing
item.description: Online video offers higher engagement with consumers, but a fresh range of challenges

item.title: Help! Our newest client is an AI model
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at the work of Rutherford Hall, critical communications strategist

item.title: Streaming services give TV adverts a new lease of life
item.description: The format is increasingly valuable as digital platforms look for new sources of revenue

item.title: CMOs tread line between maths and magic
item.description: Marketing directors now need a wide array of skills

item.title: End of the road for the ‘Mad Men’ as AI moves into advertising
item.description: Sprawling marketing groups are struggling to respond to new technology

item.title: Marketers tackle risks and opportunities on social media
item.description: Advertisers are having to work harder to ensure their messages are getting through in the right way

item.title: Brands flock to sports for added reach
item.description: Live events offer marketers the sort of high value audience that other channels struggle to match

item.title: Advertisers seek to capitalise on the promise of AI
item.description: Marketers need to balance the efficiency offered by automation with the authenticity that consumers demand

item.title: Brands look beyond search engines as customer habits shift
item.description: Consumers are finding fresh ways to look for products and services online

item.title: Should your board appoint a bot?
item.description: New AI tools help chairs and directors with prep and research but are unlikely to be granted a vote

item.title: Chip toolmaker Tokyo Electron cuts ties with executive linked to Chinese rivals
item.description: Veteran executive Jay Chen, who helped build Japanese group’s China business, left after links to start-ups surfaced

item.title: Zero-hour contract crackdown risks job losses, business warns
item.description: Trade bodies say a requirement to offer staff guaranteed time could be ‘tipping point’ in already weak labour market

item.title: FirstFT: Shooting suspect had written about targeting Trump, authorities say
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Netanyahu’s biggest rivals merge Israeli political parties, China’s Xiaomi targets European expansion

item.title: Michael Jackson biopic is hit with audiences despite critical backlash
item.description: ‘Michael’ earns $217mn on opening weekend in boon for Lionsgate, Universal and the late singer’s estate
